pairs.question: What was the significance of the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække in the history of Danish football?
pairs.answer: The 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække was the 39th season of the Copenhagen Football Championship, established in 1889, and the 25th under the administration of Kjøbenhavns Boldspil-Union (KBU). It served as a qualifier for the national championship final of the 1926–27 Landsfodboldturneringen and marked the debut of the new Danish Championship play-off structure in the following season.

pairs.question: Which team won the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække, and what additional title did they secure that fall?
pairs.answer: B.93 won the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække, claiming their sixth KBU league title. During the fall of 1926, they also won the championship trophy of the 1926 KBUs Pokalturnering, achieving a double.

pairs.question: Who was the top goalscorer in the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække, and how many goals did they score?
pairs.answer: Pauli Jørgensen from BK Frem was the league's top goalscorer, scoring 10 goals during the 1926–27 season.

pairs.question: What was the outcome for BK Fremad Amager in the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække despite finishing last?
pairs.answer: Despite finishing last in their debut season, BK Fremad Amager secured their spot in the next season of the KBUs Mesterskabsrække by winning the first promotion/relegation play-off match against the winner of the 1926–27 KBUs A-række, Handelsstandens BK.

pairs.question: How did the winner of the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække qualify for the national championship?
pairs.answer: The winner of the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække automatically qualified for the national championship final of the 1926–27 Landsfodboldturneringen, which was played at the end of the regular season.

pairs.question: What new structure was introduced in Danish football following the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække?
pairs.answer: The 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække was the final season before the introduction of the 1927–28 Danmarksmesterskabsturneringen i Fodbold, a new Danish Championship play-off structure that all six teams from the league participated in during its inaugural season.

pairs.question: Which teams automatically qualified for the 1927 edition of the KBUs Pokalturnering?
pairs.answer: The six teams from the 1926–27 KBUs Mesterskabsrække, including the winners and runners-up of the second-tier league (1926–27 KBUs A-række), automatically qualified for the 1927 edition of the KBUs Pokalturnering.
